assertion.title: Epsilon-lexicase selection + inv operator improves exact matches from 29/50 to 34/50
assertion.statement: Replacing tournament selection with epsilon-lexicase selection (MAD-based epsilon, down-sampled to 50 cases per selection event) and adding an inv (1/x) unary operator improves exact matches from 29/50 to 34/50 (+17%) and mean R² from 0.968 to 0.985. Tier 4 improves from 5/18 to 8/18. New exact matches: eq20 (Lens Equation), eq35 (Damped Oscillation), eq38 (Projectile Range), eq41 (Wave Superposition), eq43 (RC Circuit Discharge), eq45 (Standing Wave). Lost eq44 (Boltzmann Distribution, stochastic). Lexicase selection maintains structural diversity by evaluating individuals on random orderings of training cases, preventing premature convergence on approximate solutions. The inv operator directly contributed to eq41's solution (using div/inv to express multiplication by A). The combination is particularly effective for tier 4 equations requiring precise operator composition.

changes_made: Replaced tournament selection with epsilon-lexicase selection using MAD-based epsilon thresholds
Down-sampled lexicase to 50 cases per selection for efficiency
Added inv (1/x) unary operator for reciprocal patterns
Precompute per-case error matrix once per generation for efficient selection
Aggregate fitness still used for elitism (top-5 survive)
